this is what I always thought too, but Denis explained a couple days ago that it’s not actually accurate. The line is the most likely track and the cone is the margin of error for that track. Meaning that the line (or the eye) could actually be anywhere within the cone. That’s why the cone is bigger further away from the storm (because there’s a bigger margin of error further away from the storm) and smaller closer to the storm. If the cone was just hurricane like conditions, it would be bigger closest to the storm (in a circle).
They factor these things into the track currently. All the best
